evidence_excerpt: Long-term course showed worsening visual impairment and persistent palinopsia, with cerebellar atrophy that 'had only slightly increased over 7 years'; seizures became controlled but visual and neurologic features progressed.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Ongoing severe visual loss and 'transient but protracted visual disturbances consistent with palinopsia' lasting days; mild depression noted.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Antiepileptic treatment with levetiracetam and valproate controlled his seizures.
He repeatedly experienced long-lasting episodes of palinopsia; fVEPs after onset showed a further decline and polyphasic waves.
Visual impairment worsened to severe by age 29 (previously visual acuity 1/20).
MRI at age 22 showed severe cerebellar atrophy that 'had only slightly increased over 7 years.'

notes_on_uncertainty: Record describes two siblings; this summary focuses on Patient 1, who has the most detailed longitudinal data. Exact diagnosis date is not stated; the first specialist evaluation (referral for poor seizure control around mid-20s, with skin biopsy at 25) was used as the index. Timing of palinopsia onset relative to index is approximate.
